input: Variance (sigma squared): Numerical measure of spread. Squares differences from mean to ensure positive values. Population variance: sigma^2 = sum((X - mu)^2) / N. Sample variance: s^2 = sum((X - xbar)^2) / (N-1). NumPy: np.var(data) for population, np.var(data, ddof=1) for sample. Small variance = clustered near mean, large variance = spread out. Manual steps: compute mean, subtract from each value, square differences, average. Related: standard deviation, mean, normal distribution.
